saints.en: St. Prophet Joel. St. Martyr Varus. Ven. John of Rila

rubrics:
Вечірня: катизми нема. На «Господи, взиваю» 6 стихир: 3 прор., 3 мч., Сл., І нині: богород. Прокімен дня. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар прор., Сл.: мч., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря мч. і за днем тижня. Відпуст середній з помин. прор. і мч.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар прор. (2), Сл.: мч., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря мч. Катизми чергові, сідальні Октоїха. Канон Октоїха, прор. і мч. На 9-ій пісні – «Чеснішу від херувимів». По 9-ій – світильний Октоїха, прор., Сл.: мч., І нині: богород.. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар прор., Сл.: мч., І нині: богород. за гласом тропаря мч. і за днем тижня. Відпуст середній з помин. прор. і мч.
Часи: на 1-му – тропар дня; на 6-му – храму; на 3-му – прор. та 9-му – мч.
Літургія: вся Служба – дня. Ап. – Кол. 255 зач.; 2, 13-20. Єв. – Лк. 43 зач.; 9, 18-22.

troparion_en.text: Your life was a foundation of repentance / and a model of compunction, / an image of consolation and spiritual perfection, / equal to the angels, O venerable one. / You persevered in prayer, fasting and tears, O Father John; / intercede with Christ God for our souls.

kontakion_en.text: Imitating the life of the angels and forsaking all earthly things, / you fled to Christ, O venerable one. / Protecting yourself with His commandments, / you were shown to be an unshakable pillar against the attacks of the enemy. / Therefore, we cry to you: “Rejoice, O Father John, most brilliant light!”

matched_oca_saint: Translation of the relics of Venerable John, Abbot of Rila, Bulgaria

troparion.text: We celebrate the memory / of Your prophet Joel, O Lord; / through him we entreat You, / save our souls.

kontakion.text: (Podoben: “Today You have shown forth...”)Enlightened by the Spirit, your pure heart became the dwelling place of most splendid prophecy; / for you saw things far off as if they were near.  / Therefore, we honor you, blessed and glorious Prophet Joel.

oca_saints.name: Martyr Varus, and seven Monastic Martyrs with him

troparion.text: Seeing the host of holy passion-bearers suffering under the law,  / you courageously revealed your strength,  / and voluntarily hastened to suffer, desiring to die for Christ;  / accepting the reward of your victorious martyrdom, O Varus,  / pray for the salvation of our souls.

kontakion.text: Following Christ, O Martyr Varus, you drank of His cup;1  /  you received the crown of martyrdom and you rejoice with the Angels. /  Pray unceasingly for our souls.

oca_saints.name: Translation of the relics of Venerable John, Abbot of Rila, Bulgaria

troparion.text: Your life was a foundation of repentance / and a model of compunction, / an image of consolation and spiritual perfection, / equal to the angels, O venerable one. / You persevered in prayer, fasting and tears, O Father John; / intercede with Christ God for our souls.

kontakion.text: Imitating the life of the angels and forsaking all earthly things, / you fled to Christ, O venerable one. / Protecting yourself with His commandments, / you were shown to be an unshakable pillar against the attacks of the enemy. / Therefore, we cry to you: “Rejoice, O Father John, most brilliant light!”

oca_saints.name: Hieromartyr Sadoc (Sadoth), Bishop of Persia, and 128 Martyrs with him

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

O dweller of the desert and angel in the body, / you were shown to be a wonderworker, our Godbearing Father Prochorus. / You received heavenly gifts through fasting, vigil, and prayer; / healing the sick and the souls of those drawn to you by faith. / Glory to Him who gave you strength! / Glory to Him who granted you a crown! / Glory to Him who through you grants healing to all!

kontakion.text: Boldly revealing a great victory and burning with heavenly love, / you disdained the corruptible king and rejected all earthly pleasures; / taking up your abode on Mount Kozjak, / you ascended from it to the King of kings in heaven. / O Prochorus, ceaselessly intercede for us all!
